template.name: consumer_deposit_allocation
template.description:
Allocate a matched consumer's deposit by reclassifying the liability from the per-file Unallocated Customer (20900) to the consumer's Hold account (20101) on the economic ledger, and from the per-file Unallocated Customer FBO (21900) to the consumer's FBO Clearing (21101) on the allocation ledger. Fired by the reconciliation service after resolving a consumer_external_id to real account IDs. Funds go to Hold (not Available) because the bank statement may not have arrived yet.

The template also carries consumer_available (20100) and consumer_fbo_main (21100) as pass-through accounts. These are not moved by T5104 but are persisted in the event's accounts array so that the downstream T5402 consumer release template can read them without a separate account resolution query.

financial_statements_impact.description: Liability-to-liability reclassification on the economic ledger: Unallocated Customer (20900) decreases and Consumer Hold (20101) increases by the same amount. Total liabilities unchanged.

Liability-to-liability reclassification on the allocation ledger: Unallocated Customer FBO (21900) decreases and Consumer FBO Clearing (21101) increases by the same amount. Total liabilities unchanged.

template.trigger: Reconciliation service resolves a consumer_external_id from the allocation file to real account IDs and allocates the deposit. Fires once per matched consumer per file. Can fire before the bank statement arrives.

ledger_scope_descriptions.operational: Operational ledger (ID 5001) where the unallocated deposit liability is reclassified to a specific consumer's hold account.
ledger_scope_descriptions.fdic_tracking: FDIC tracking ledger (ID 5002) where the unallocated FDIC custodial position is reclassified to the consumer's FBO clearing account.

accounting_treatments.description: Reclassify the deposit liability from the aggregate unallocated pool to a specific consumer's hold account on the economic ledger; total deposit obligations unchanged.
Reclassify the FDIC custodial position from the unallocated pool to the consumer's FBO clearing account on the allocation ledger.

account_code_validations.description: Per-file Unallocated Customer liability on the economic ledger; debited to release the unallocated deposit for this consumer.
Consumer deposit hold liability on the economic ledger; credited with the allocation amount (funds on hold pending bank confirmation).
Consumer available deposit liability on the economic ledger; pass-through account carried for downstream T5402 consumer release (not moved by T5104).
Per-file Unallocated Customer FBO liability on the allocation ledger; debited to release the unallocated FDIC position for this consumer.
Consumer FBO Clearing liability on the allocation ledger; credited to establish the consumer's FDIC custodial position in clearing status.
Consumer FBO Main liability on the allocation ledger; pass-through account carried for downstream T5402 consumer release (not moved by T5104).

consumer_external_id.description: Consumer entity external identifier (now resolved to real account IDs in the accounts array).

line_reference.description: Link back to the detail line (T5103 event) being allocated for auditability.

statement_file_id.description: Bank statement file reference for traceability and reconciliation pairing. Optional because T5104 can fire before the bank statement arrives.

clearing_account_id.description: Optional clearing account identifier for traceability when the allocation is associated with a specific clearing account.

marketplace.description: White-labelled product line identifier for this allocation (e.g. CraftNest, QuickBite, SkillForge, SeatVault, StayLocal). Optional — used for analytics and audit traceability.

payment_rail.description: Inbound payment rail that originated the deposit (e.g. ACH, WIRE, PUSH_TO_DEBIT, PAYPAL). Optional — used for analytics and reconciliation traceability.

allocation_file_id.description: Allocation file reference used to select the per-file Unallocated Customer (20900/21900) instances being drawn down.

legs.description: Reclassify the deposit from the aggregate unallocated pool to this specific consumer's hold account pending bank confirmation.
Reclassify the FDIC custodial position from the unallocated pool to this consumer's FBO clearing account.

examples.name: consumer_a_match_8000
examples.description: Reconciliation service matches consumer-A (cust-a-001) and reclassifies $8,000.00 from the per-file Unallocated Customer to Consumer A Hold. Carries consumer_available and consumer_fbo_main as pass-through accounts for downstream T5402.

examples.name: consumer_b_match_2000
examples.description: Reconciliation service matches consumer-B (cust-b-001) and reclassifies $2,000.00 from the per-file Unallocated Customer to Consumer B Hold. Carries consumer_available and consumer_fbo_main as pass-through accounts for downstream T5402.
